originalTitle: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
summary: "Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to […]
content: “Group project, but make it 1776.” That’s how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google’s collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to check on the status of a draft, who takes a photo and uses AI to transcribe it into a Google Doc. Franklin and Adams hop in to make edits in suggestion mode, Gemini finds them a meeting time, takes notes during a Google Meet call, and then Nano Banana whips up a seal for the United States featuring a turkey (probably the more honest choice, instead of an eagle). The last thing before the fireworks go off, the founding fathers ask Gemini for its advice on whether to give King George III edit access to the Declaration of Independence. Which should make Americans of all political stripes want to throw their phones out a window. (I wonder what Gemini’s reaction would have been if the founding fathers had asked about women’s voting rights, slavery, or Manifest Destiny?) The whole thing is ill-advised, corny, and just plain dumb. As CUNY history professor Angus Johnston said on Bluesky, “Even in a corny fantasy joke, it’s impossible to make the case that AI is a useful tool for political organizing, writing, or human collaboration.” [Media: https://youtu.be/Q3RjZY-rSsc]
chineseTitle: 令人愤怒的谷歌广告想象创始人拥抱 AI
chineseSummary: “集体项目，但定在1776年。”这就是 Google Workspace 的新广告的开头。从那时起事情只会变得更加令人毛骨悚然。该片段想象了如果开国元勋们求助于谷歌的协作工具和 Gemini 来帮助他们起草《独立宣言》会是什么样子。本·富兰克林给托马斯·杰斐逊发短信给[...]
chineseContent: “集体项目，但定在 1776 年。”这就是 Google Workspace 的新广告的开头。从那时起事情只会变得更加令人毛骨悚然。该片段想象了如果开国元勋们求助于谷歌的协作工具和 Gemini 来帮助他们起草《独立宣言》会是什么样子。本·富兰克林 (Ben Franklin) 给托马斯·杰斐逊 (Thomas Jefferson) 发短信，询问草稿的状态，杰斐逊拍了一张照片，并使用人工智能将其转录为 Google 文档。富兰克林和亚当斯以建议模式进行编辑，双子座为他们找到了一个会议时间，在 Google Meet 通话期间做笔记，然后 Nano Banana 为美国制作了一个印有火鸡的印章（可能是更诚实的选择，而不是鹰）。烟花燃放前的最后一件事是，开国元勋们向双子座询问是否允许乔治三世国王编辑《独立宣言》的建议。这应该会让所有政治派别的美国人都想把手机扔出窗外。 （我想知道如果开国元勋们问起妇女的投票权、奴隶制或昭昭命运，双子座会作何反应？）整件事都是不明智的、老套的，而且简直是愚蠢的。正如纽约市立大学历史学教授安格斯·约翰斯顿 (Angus Johnston) 在 Bluesky 上所说，“即使在一个老生常谈的幻想笑话中，也不可能证明人工智能是政治组织、写作或人类协作的有用工具。” [媒体：https://youtu.be/Q3RjZY-rSsc]
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
